abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems become increasingly integrated into the fabric of daily life, they present a dual reality: significant opportunities for innovation alongside substantial risks to individual and societal well-being. To mitigate these risks, global regulators are developing diverse normative frameworks. While many jurisdictions have relied on non-binding ethics guidelines and recommendations, the European Union (EU) has taken a decisive step toward formal oversight. In early 2024, the EU adopted the Artificial Intelligence Act, a landmark regulation that entered into force on August 1, 2024, with its core provisions set to take effect within two years. Distinguishing itself through a systematic, horizontal approach that regulates AI across all sectors rather than focusing on niche applications, the AI Act is widely recognized as the first comprehensive legal instrument of its kind. Given its status as a global trailblazer and its broad extraterritorial reach, the Act is poised to fundamentally shape the development and deployment of AI both within Europe and across the international stage.
